headlineSummary: OpenAI drew clearly positive sentiment, while Anthropic was mixed-neutral with strong praise offset by criticism about code use and trust [E1][E3][E6].

ranking.summary: Anthropic landed in mixed-neutral territory: some comments praised Claude’s code and visual reasoning, but others criticized Claude Code in shared projects and questioned its trustworthiness [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5].

OpenAI was viewed positively, with commenters saying Codex and GPT5.4 helped speed development and showed useful spatial understanding, though the praise was measured rather than overwhelming [E6][E7][E8].

evidence.excerpt: Claude understood the problem and produced elegant Python code that worked perfectly the first time.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly credits Claude with understanding the task and producing code that worked perfectly on the first try, which is strong praise of capability.

evidence.excerpt: My mind-blown moment was when I was doing work like this and Claude started positioning the camera itself to get better looks at areas it wanted to improve.

annotations.rationale: "mind-blown moment" and the described autonomous camera adjustment convey strong admiration for Claude's visual reasoning ability.

evidence.excerpt: this 19k LoC PR was mostly generated by Claude Code and manually reviewed by the submitter which in my opinion is against the spirit of the project and directly violates the terms of Developer's Certificate of Origin

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Claude Code-generated contribution is against project spirit and directly violates contributor rules, which is strong criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Using Claude for code you use yourself or at your own company internally is one thing, but when you start injecting it into widely-shared projects like this (or, the linux kernel, or Debian, etc) there will always be a lingering feeling of the project being tainted.

annotations.rationale: The phrase "project being tainted" expresses strong distrust and disapproval of using Claude in shared upstream projects.

evidence.excerpt: What is clear is that it's a huge productivity boost over vanilla Claude Code.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says GSD is a huge productivity boost over vanilla Claude Code, implying Claude Code performs worse in comparison.

evidence.excerpt: We used AI and Codex heavily for this project, as otherwise the timeline would have spanned to a year plus to develop.

annotations.rationale: The author explicitly credits Codex with dramatically accelerating development, indicating strong approval of its usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: I've observed that GPT5.4 can iteratively position the perspective camera and stop once it reaches subjectively interesting arrangements. I don't know how to quantify this, but it does seem to have some sense of world space.

annotations.rationale: The commenter attributes useful spatial behavior to GPT5.4 and says it seems to have some sense of world space, which is mild praise.

evidence.excerpt: Mostly the text debugging outputs are very helpful to Codex. It will often add debugging payloads when we're focused on a particular issue. I do occasionally take screenshots and paste them into Codex and explain the issue that I'm seeing. It seems to understand a certain amount, especially if the issue can be seen in orthogonal views.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Codex is very helpful and can understand visual issues to some extent, which is clear mild praise.
